Title: Отображение статистики зависимых от registry приложений
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Design registry data model & storage
Description:
Design entities and storage for shadcn/rss registry tracking.

**Goal**
Have a clear data model to store registries, components, and changes (history), ready to be used by fetchers, diff engine, and UI.

**Scope**

* Define entities: `Registry`, `Component`, `Change` (and related helpers if needed).
* Decide how to store change history (append-only log vs snapshots + derived views).
* Add schema/migrations in the codebase.

**Acceptance criteria**

* ER diagram or equivalent schema documented in the repo.
* Database schema/migrations merged.
* Example records can be inserted without ambiguity.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement registry fetcher for trusted registries
Description:
Implement a service that fetches data from trusted shadcn registries and normalizes it into the internal data model.

**Goal**
Have a reliable fetcher that can pull the current state of each trusted registry on demand.

**Scope**

* Read registry list (e.g. directory.json / config) with URLs and metadata.
* Implement HTTP client with basic retry / timeout strategy.
* Normalize remote data into `Registry`, `Component` (and related) entities.
* Store the fetched snapshot in the database.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Running the fetcher locally populates the database for all configured trusted registries.
* Errors are logged with enough context (which registry, which URL, status code).
* At least one simple test registry is used in automated tests.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement change detection pipeline (diff engine)
Description:
Implement a diff engine that compares previous and current registry snapshots and produces `Change` records.

**Goal**
Be able to detect added/updated/removed components (and other relevant changes) between two points in time.

**Scope**

* Decide what constitutes a “change” (add / remove / update, and minimal fields to track).
* Implement comparison between two snapshots for a single registry.
* Produce `Change` entities with type, timestamp, registry, component, and payload.
* Handle idempotency: re-running diff should not duplicate already-processed changes.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Given two sample snapshots, diff engine produces predictable `Change` records.
* Unit tests cover add / update / remove scenarios.
* Diff output is stored and can be queried via the data layer.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Expose public API endpoint to list registry changes
Description:
Add a public API endpoint to query registry changes with basic filters.

**Goal**
Allow the frontend (and later external consumers) to fetch changes in a structured way.

**Scope**

* Add endpoint (e.g. `GET /api/changes`).
* Support filters: `registry`, `from` / `to` date, `changeType` (add/update/remove).
* Implement pagination (cursor or page/limit).
* Return a stable, documented response shape.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Endpoint returns changes from the database for at least one real registry.
* Filters work and are covered by tests (at least minimal integration tests).
* Basic API docs / README section added.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Build basic UI for the Registry Change Feed
Description:
Implement the initial UI that shows a feed of registry changes with minimal filtering.

**Goal**
Provide a simple but usable view where users can see what changed in registries.

**Scope**

* Page with a list of changes (date, registry name, change type, short summary).
* Filters: registry select, date range.
* Loading / empty / error states.
* Link each change to the registry detail (or external GitHub/PR link if available).

**Acceptance criteria**

* Page is accessible from main navigation.
* A user can filter by one registry and see only its changes.
* Reasonable performance on initial dataset.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add scheduler & logging for fetch + diff jobs
Description:
Set up a minimal scheduling mechanism to periodically fetch registry data and run the diff engine, with logging for observability.

**Goal**
Keep the change feed reasonably fresh without manual triggers.

**Scope**

* Add scheduled job (cron/queue) to run fetch + diff for all trusted registries.
* Ensure jobs are safe to re-run (idempotent or guarded).
* Log summary per run: how many registries processed, how many changes detected, how many errors.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Job can be triggered locally (e.g. via CLI / dev command) and runs end-to-end.
* Logs clearly show progress and failures.
* No duplicate `Change` records when the job is re-run for the same data.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Classify change severity (breaking / minor / docs-only)
Description:
Add a basic classification layer on top of raw `Change` records to mark their severity.

**Goal**
Make the change feed more meaningful by labeling changes by impact level.

**Scope**

* Define simple heuristics for: `breaking`, `minor`, `docs-only` (or equivalent).
* Extend `Change` model to include `severity` field.
* Apply classification in the diff pipeline or a post-processing step.
* Surface severity in API and UI.

**Acceptance criteria**

* `Change` entities include a `severity` value.
* UI shows severity (e.g. badge) per change.
* Tests cover at least a few example changes for each severity type.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add seed data and fixtures for local development
Description:
Provide a small, realistic dataset for local development and tests so the change feed is easy to work on.

**Goal**
Make it easy for contributors to run the app locally and immediately see changes in the feed.

**Scope**

* Add fixtures for 1–2 registries with multiple snapshots.
* Script/command to seed the database with registries, components, and changes.
* Ensure seeds don’t conflict with production config.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Running a single command seeds local DB with demo registries and changes.
* The UI shows a non-empty change feed after seeding.
* Docs mention how to run seeds.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Document MVP architecture and usage for the registry change feed
Description:
Write documentation that explains how the registry change feed works and how to work with it as a developer.

**Goal**
Make it clear how the system is structured (fetch → diff → API → UI) and how to extend it.

**Scope**

* High-level architecture diagram or markdown section.
* Describe data flow: fetcher → snapshots → diff engine → changes → API → UI.
* Add instructions for running jobs, seeding data, and accessing the UI locally.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Docs live in the repo (`README` or `/docs`), linked from root.
* A new contributor can read the docs and understand where to plug new registries or change logic.
* MVP responsibilities and limitations are clearly stated.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Extend data model with registry metadata & health stats
Description:
Extend the existing data model to store metadata and simple health metrics for each registry.

**Goal**
Support a public directory that can show per-registry info (type, status, activity/health).

**Scope**

* Add fields for: type (trusted/community), homepage URL, GitHub repo, description, tags.
* Add computed fields or separate table for basic metrics: last change date, number of changes in N days, health score.
* Backfill data for existing registries.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Registry entities contain all required metadata fields.
* Basic health metrics are computed and persisted or derivable efficiently.
* Existing registries show non-null metadata where available.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement registry health scoring logic
Description:
Define and implement a simple scoring system that ranks registries by “health” / activity.

**Goal**
Provide a single, understandable signal to surface in the public directory (active / stable / low activity).

**Scope**

* Define what goes into the score (e.g. last update recency, number of changes in last 30/90 days).
* Map numeric score to labels: “Highly active”, “Stable”, “Low activity”, etc.
* Store score + label for each registry and update it on schedule.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Each registry has a `healthScore` and `healthLabel`.
* Health labels align with expectations for a few hand-picked registries (manual spot check).
* Score recomputes correctly when new changes land.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Build public registries directory page (/registries)
Description:
Implement the main directory page listing all registries with key metadata and health signals.

**Goal**
Make shadcn/rss the default way to discover and compare registries at a glance.

**Scope**

* Page at `/registries` with list/table/grid of registries.
* Show: name, type (trusted/community), health label, last update date, short description.
* Add basic filters: by type, by health label, search by name.
* Loading/empty/error states.

**Acceptance criteria**

* `/registries` lists all active registries from the DB.
* Filters and search work as expected.
* Page feels responsive on initial dataset.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add registry detail page with change timeline
Description:
Create a detail page for an individual registry that shows metadata and its change history.

**Goal**
Provide a shareable URL for each registry with both a summary and a timeline of changes.

**Scope**

* Route like `/registry/[slug]` (human-readable slug based on name/id).
* Show full metadata: name, type, description, links (docs, GitHub, demo).
* Include a timeline of recent changes (powered by existing change feed API).
* Link to external resources (e.g. GitHub repo, registry homepage).

**Acceptance criteria**

* Each registry has a stable URL and slug.
* Visiting a registry page displays its metadata and recent changes.
* Navigating from the directory to a registry page works smoothly.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add visual activity indicators to registry pages
Description:
Add lightweight visualizations to better communicate registry activity over time.

**Goal**
Make registry activity easier to understand than raw lists of changes.

**Scope**

* On the registry detail page, add a simple chart or sparkline for changes over time (e.g. per week/month).
* Optionally add a compact indicator on the `/registries` list (e.g. tiny sparkline per row).
* Re-use existing change data; avoid heavy/complex charting.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Activity chart renders for registries with sufficient data.
* No significant performance degradation on the directory page.
* Visual indicators align with the health score/label (no obvious contradictions).
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Expose API endpoints for registry directory and details
Description:
Add API endpoints to power the directory and detail pages, and to allow future consumers (agents/integrations) to query registry info.

**Goal**
Provide a clean, versionable API surface for registries.

**Scope**

* `GET /api/registries` with filters (type, health, search, pagination).
* `GET /api/registries/[id-or-slug]` for a single registry with metadata + basic stats.
* Ensure responses are consistent and documented.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Frontend uses these endpoints (no duplicated logic in client).
* API responses include all data needed for current UI.
* Docs or schema for both endpoints are added.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Improve SEO and sharing for registry directory & detail pages
Description:
Optimize registry pages for discovery and sharing.

**Goal**
Make `/registries` and `/registry/[slug]` discoverable via search and nice to share on social.

**Scope**

* Set descriptive `<title>` and meta description for directory and detail pages.
* Add Open Graph / Twitter card tags for registry detail pages (name, description, type).
* Ensure URLs are clean, stable, and indexable.

**Acceptance criteria**

* View source / devtools shows correct meta tags for a sample registry.
* Sharing a registry URL in X/Discord generates a reasonable preview.
* Directory and detail pages don’t expose obviously broken SEO metadata.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add tests & QA checklist for registry directory
Description:
Cover the directory and registry pages with basic tests and document a minimal QA checklist.

**Goal**
Keep the directory stable as new registries and features are added.

**Scope**

* Add unit/integration tests for directory filtering/search and registry detail fetching.
* Add at least one end-to-end flow: user opens `/registries`, filters, opens a registry, sees timeline.
* Document a short manual QA checklist for new registry-related changes.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Tests pass and cover main happy paths.
* CI fails if core directory behaviour is broken.
* QA checklist lives in the repo and is referenced from the project docs.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Backfill and sync job for registry metadata & stats
Description:
Ensure all registries have up-to-date metadata and health stats via a periodic sync job.

**Goal**
Keep directory data fresh without manual updates.

**Scope**

* Script/command to backfill metadata and health stats for existing registries.
* Periodic job to recompute stats (health score, last update, counts).
* Log summary of how many registries were updated and any failures.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Running the job updates stats for all registries without errors (or with logged errors).
* New registries start appearing in `/registries` with reasonable defaults.
* Directory doesn’t show obviously stale stats after sync.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Document registry directory and how to add/update registries
Description:
Add documentation for how the registry directory works and how to add/update registries as a maintainer.

**Goal**
Make it easy for future contributors (and future you) to understand and extend the directory.

**Scope**

* Describe data model: registry metadata, health stats, activity charts.
* Explain how `/registries` and `/registry/[slug]` are powered (API, caching, jobs).
* Add a step-by-step guide for adding a new registry or updating metadata.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Docs live in the repo (`/docs` or main README) and are easy to find.
* A new contributor can follow the docs and successfully add/update a registry.
* Directory limitations and future ideas (nice-to-haves) are briefly noted.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Design user & watchlist data model
Description:
Design the data model for users and their registry watchlists.

**Goal**
Support authenticated users with a persistent list of registries they follow.

**Scope**

* Define `User` entity (GitHub-based identity, minimal profile fields).
* Define `Watchlist` / `UserRegistry` relationship (which registries a user follows).
* Decide how to store future per-user settings (notifications, preferences).
* Add migrations/schema to the codebase.

**Acceptance criteria**

* User & watchlist entities are defined and documented.
* Migrations/schema are merged and can be applied.
* A user can be associated with multiple registries (and vice versa).
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement GitHub OAuth login
Description:
Add GitHub OAuth as the primary authentication method for shadcn/rss.

**Goal**
Allow users to log in with GitHub so we can attach watchlists and future settings to an account.

**Scope**

* Configure GitHub OAuth app and environment variables.
* Implement login/logout flow in the app.
* On first login, create a `User` record; on subsequent logins, reuse it.
* Store minimal profile data (id, username, avatar, email if available).

**Acceptance criteria**

* Users can log in and log out using GitHub.
* A `User` record exists for logged-in users.
* Basic error handling for failed/denied auth is in place
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add backend endpoints for managing user watchlists
Description:
Add API endpoints that allow users to manage which registries they follow.

**Goal**
Provide a simple CRUD API for watchlists to power the UI.

**Scope**

* `GET /api/me/watchlist` – list registries the user follows.
* `POST /api/me/watchlist` – add a registry to watchlist.
* `DELETE /api/me/watchlist/:registryId` – remove a registry from watchlist.
* All endpoints require authentication and are scoped to the current user.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Authenticated user can add/remove registries from their watchlist via API.
* Duplicate entries are prevented or handled gracefully (idempotency).
* API returns clear errors for unauthenticated or invalid requests.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Build login entry point and "My Watchlist" page
Description:
Implement the UI pieces for logging in and managing a personal watchlist.

**Goal**
Give users a clear way to sign in and see/edit their watchlist.

**Scope**

* Add login/logout controls in the main navigation (GitHub button).
* Add a “My Watchlist” page listing followed registries.
* Allow adding/removing registries from the watchlist (e.g. toggle/star).
* Show states: empty watchlist, loading, error.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Logged-out users see a prompt to log in to use watchlists.
* Logged-in users can see a list of their followed registries.
* Toggling follow/unfollow updates both UI and backend.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Integrate watchlist into the change feed (My registries filter)
Description:
Connect user watchlists with the change feed so users can see updates only for registries they follow.

**Goal**
Make the change feed feel personal and reduce noise.

**Scope**

* Add an option like “My registries” / “Watchlist only” to the change feed.
* When enabled, only show changes for registries on the user’s watchlist.
* Provide a sensible fallback for logged-out users (e.g. disable the toggle).

**Acceptance criteria**

* Logged-in users can filter the feed to only their registries.
* Switching between “All registries” and “My registries” works as expected.
* Logged-out users don’t see broken UI for this feature.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Track read/unread status for changes per user
Description:
Add data structures to track which changes a user has already seen/read.

**Goal**
Enable unread indicators and “inbox-like” experience for registry changes.

**Scope**

* Design and implement a `UserChangeState` (or similar) entity to store read/unread per user+change.
* Decide on default state (all new changes start as unread).
* Ensure the model can scale reasonably (e.g. pruning old records).

**Acceptance criteria**

* For a given user and change, the system can answer: is this read or unread?
* New changes for a watched registry appear as unread by default.
* Basic tests cover creation and querying of read/unread state.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add unread indicators and mark-as-read interactions in the feed
Description:
Surface read/unread state in the UI and let users mark changes as read.

**Goal**
Make the change feed feel like an inbox, not just a raw log.

**Scope**

* Show unread indicator for changes (e.g. dot/badge) in the feed.
* Allow users to mark a single change as read.
* Optionally, add “Mark all as read” for the current view / registry.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Unread changes are visually distinct from read ones.
* Marking a change as read updates both backend and UI.
* “Mark all as read” (if implemented) behaves predictably and doesn’t break state.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add foundation for per-user notification preferences
Description:
Prepare the data model to store per-user notification preferences, even if actual delivery (email/Telegram/etc.) comes later.

**Goal**
Make it possible to later plug in notification channels without changing the user/Watchlist core.

**Scope**

* Add a `NotificationPreferences` (or similar) model linked to `User`.
* Fields for: preferred channels (email/none), frequency (realtime/daily/weekly), and scope (all changes / breaking only / watchlist only).
* Add default preferences for new users.

**Acceptance criteria**

* A user has a persisted notification preferences object.
* Preferences can be read/updated via backend (even if no UI yet).
* Defaults are sane and documented.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add tests & QA checklist for accounts & watchlist
Description:
Cover the main account and watchlist flows with automated tests and a minimal QA checklist.

**Goal**
Make sure login + watchlist + read/unread behaviour stays stable over time.

**Scope**

* Tests for: GitHub login, creating a user, creating/removing watchlist entries.
* Tests for: My Watchlist page behaviour (empty, non-empty, permissions).
* Tests for: read/unread state and “My registries” filter integration.
* Write a short manual QA checklist for account/watchlist features.

**Acceptance criteria**

* CI covers main happy paths for auth + watchlist + feed integration.
* Breaking changes to these flows are caught by tests.
* QA checklist lives in the repo and is easy to find.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Document accounts, watchlists, and personal feed behaviour
Description:
Document how accounts, watchlists, and per-user feed behaviour work from both user and developer perspectives.

**Goal**
Make it easy to understand what “logging in” gives you and how to work with watchlists in code.

**Scope**

* User-facing overview: what you get with an account (watchlist, personal feed, unread markers).
* Dev-facing notes: data model (User, Watchlist, UserChangeState, NotificationPreferences), key APIs.
* Instructions: how to run auth locally (GitHub app config), and how to test watchlist flows.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Docs are added to README or `/docs` and referenced from the project.
* A new contributor can read and understand how to extend account-related features.
* Behaviour/limitations for logged-out vs logged-in users are clearly described.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Design notification events and delivery model
Description:
Design the core model for notification events and deliveries for shadcn/rss.

**Goal**
Have a clear abstraction for “what happened” (events) and “how/when it was sent” (deliveries) independent of channels.

**Scope**

* Define `NotificationEvent` (e.g. new change, breaking change, new registry on watchlist).
* Define `NotificationDelivery` (channel, status, timestamp, error if any).
* Decide how events are generated (sync/async) and how deliveries are scheduled.
* Add DB schema/migrations.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Data model for events & deliveries is documented and merged.
* It’s possible to record “this user should be notified about change X on channel Y”.
* Model does not depend on a specific channel implementation.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Generate notification events from change pipeline
Description:
Hook into the change detection pipeline to generate notification events based on user watchlists and preferences.

**Goal**
Automatically create events whenever something happens that a user opted into.

**Scope**

* For each new `Change`, determine affected users (watchlists).
* Apply basic preference filters (e.g. breaking-only, watchlist-only).
* Create `NotificationEvent` records for those users.
* Ensure idempotency (no duplicate events if job re-runs for same change).

**Acceptance criteria**

* When a new change is stored, corresponding events are created for relevant users.
* Re-running the job for the same change does not duplicate events.
* Unit tests cover simple scenarios (1 user, many users, no users).
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement email delivery worker (digest + realtime foundations)
Description:
Implement an email delivery worker that can send notifications based on queued events, starting with a simple digest.

**Goal**
Send actual emails using existing notification preferences (daily/weekly to start).

**Scope**

* Integrate with chosen email provider (SMTP/service) and config.
* Implement worker that reads pending `NotificationEvent` records and groups them into email payloads per user.
* Support at least daily digest (e.g. “changes for your watchlist in the last 24h”).
* Record `NotificationDelivery` outcomes (success/failure).

**Acceptance criteria**

* Running the worker produces at least one real email in a dev/test environment.
* Deliveries are logged as `success` or `failed` with error info.
* Basic retry/backoff behaviour is defined (even if minimal).
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add webhook delivery for project automation
Description:
Allow users to configure a webhook endpoint that will receive change notifications for their watchlist.

**Goal**
Make it easy to integrate shadcn/rss with custom scripts, bots, or other tools.

**Scope**

* Add model/API to store per-user webhook config (URL, secret, enabled flag).
* Implement a worker that sends POST requests for relevant events.
* Include minimal payload (registry, component, change type, severity, timestamp).
* Verify signatures or provide a simple HMAC-based mechanism using the shared secret.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Users can save a webhook URL and secret via UI or API.
* When a change event is created, a webhook POST is sent (if enabled).
* Failed webhooks are logged; repeated failures eventually disable the webhook (or mark as unhealthy).
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Build UI for notification settings
Description:
Add a settings page where users can control when and how they get notifications.

**Goal**
Give users a clear way to customize notification channels and frequency.

**Scope**

* “Notifications” section in user settings.
* Controls for: email on/off, email frequency (daily/weekly/none).
* Controls for: RSS feed (show/copy/regenerate URL).
* Controls for: webhook URL + secret + enable/disable.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Changes in the UI persist to `NotificationPreferences` and related models.
* Settings page clearly indicates which channels are active.
* Dangerous actions (e.g. regenerate RSS token) are confirmed.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Respect notification preferences in event generation and delivery
Description:
Ensure that notification events and deliveries strictly follow user preferences.

**Goal**
Avoid spamming users and ensure they receive only what they asked for.

**Scope**

* When generating events, honour scope preferences (breaking-only / all changes / specific registries).
* When delivering emails/webhooks/RSS, honour frequency and channel toggles.
* Add safeguards to avoid sending anything if all channels are disabled.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Users with email disabled do not receive emails.
* Users with “breaking only” do not get events for non-breaking changes.
* Tests cover at least a few preference combinations.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Create digest email template and copy
Description:
Design and implement a simple but readable email template for change digests.

**Goal**
Make digest emails easy to scan and pleasant to receive.

**Scope**

* Layout for digest email (group changes by registry, show severity badges, timestamps).
* Short intro copy explaining what the email is and how to change settings.
* Include links back to shadcn/rss pages (change feed, specific registry).

**Acceptance criteria**

* Digest email renders correctly in at least major email clients (Gmail, Apple Mail).
* Email includes a link to notification settings/unsubscribe flow.
* Content is concise and useful even for 5–20 changes.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add logging, observability, and basic rate limiting for notifications
Description:
Make notification delivery debuggable and prevent runaway spam scenarios.

**Goal**
Be able to understand what’s going on if notifications misbehave and cap worst-case send volume.

**Scope**

* Structured logging around event generation and delivery attempts.
* Basic metrics (counters for events created, delivered, failed) if metrics infra exists, or at least logs.
* Simple rate limits per user/channel (e.g. max N emails per day).

**Acceptance criteria**

* It’s possible to inspect logs and see how many notifications were sent and to whom.
* Delivery failures are visible (not silent).
* Rate limiting prevents obvious abuse or accidental spam loops.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Document notifications: email, and webhooks
Description:
Document how notifications work and how users can configure them.

**Goal**
Make it clear what channels exist, how to enable them, and how to integrate webhooks.

**Scope**

* User-facing docs explaining email digests, personal RSS, and webhooks.
* Developer-facing docs for webhook payload format and security (secrets, signatures).
* Notes on how to run notification workers locally.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Docs live in the repo and are linked from the app (or at least from README).
* Users can follow docs to set up their own RSS/webhook integration.
* Webhook payload is documented enough to build simple automations.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Design repo-aware data model for registry & component usage
Description:
Design the data model to represent GitHub repositories tracked by shadcn/rss and which registries/components they use.

**Goal**
Be able to say: “this repo uses these registries and these components” in a structured way.

**Scope**

* Define entities like `TrackedRepo` (GitHub repo binding), `RepoRegistryUsage`, `RepoComponentUsage`.
* Decide identifiers to store (GitHub repo id, owner/name, default branch, etc.).
* Add migrations/schema to the codebase.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Data model is documented and merged.
* A repo can be linked to multiple registries and components in the DB.
* Example records can be inserted without ambiguity.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement backend for linking GitHub repositories
Description:
Implement backend APIs to link a GitHub repository to a shadcn/rss user/account.

**Goal**
Allow authenticated users to register a GitHub repo as “tracked” inside shadcn/rss.

**Scope**

* API to list available GitHub repos for the current user (using their GitHub auth/app).
* API to create a `TrackedRepo` entry for a selected repo.
* Store minimal metadata (id, owner, name, URL, default branch).
* Ensure per-user access: users can only link repos they have access to.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Authenticated user can link at least one of their GitHub repos via API.
* Linked repos are persisted as `TrackedRepo` records.
* Unauthorized linking attempts are rejected.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Build UI for selecting and managing tracked GitHub repos
Description:
Add a UI where users can connect their GitHub repos to shadcn/rss and manage tracked ones.

**Goal**
Make it easy to pick repos to track and see what’s already connected.

**Scope**

* “Tracked repositories” section in the app (e.g. under account/settings).
* List of currently tracked repos (name, owner, status, last scan time).
* Flow to add a repo (select from GitHub list or enter manually if needed).
* Ability to un-link a repo.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Logged-in user can see which repos are tracked.
* Adding/removing a repo updates both UI and backend.
* Error/empty/loading states are handled gracefully.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Implement repo usage discovery via config file
Description:
Implement the first version of repo usage discovery using a config file inside the repo.

**Goal**
Avoid heavy static analysis by starting with an explicit config that describes which registries/components are used.

**Scope**

* Define a simple config format (e.g. `components.json`) describing used registries/components.
* Implement logic to fetch the config from GitHub (via API) for a tracked repo.
* Parse the config into `RepoRegistryUsage` / `RepoComponentUsage` records.

**Acceptance criteria**

* For a repo with a valid config file, usage records are created/updated.
* Missing/invalid config is handled with clear errors or warnings.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add optional lightweight code scanning for registry/component imports (v1)
Description:
Add an optional code scanning step that inspects repo source files for registry/component imports.

**Goal**
Provide a fallback/augmentation to config-based discovery without full static analysis complexity.

**Scope**

* Define patterns for common import styles (e.g. registry paths, known aliases).
* Fetch a subset of repo files (or specific directories) via GitHub API.
* Extract candidate registry/component usages and map them into usage records.
* Mark discovered usages as “inferred” vs “explicit” (from config).

**Acceptance criteria**

* For a sample repo, scanning identifies at least some real component usages.
* Inferred usages are clearly flagged in data.
* Performance characteristics are understood and documented (no crazy API overuse).
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Add scheduled job to refresh repo usage (config + scan)
Description:
Periodically refresh the usage information for tracked repos based on config and/or scanning.

**Goal**
Keep the mapping from repos to registries/components reasonably up to date.

**Scope**

* Job that iterates over `TrackedRepo` records and updates usage data.
* Respect rate limits and add simple batching/throttling.
* Log summary: number of repos processed, successes/failures.

**Acceptance criteria**

* Running the job updates usage for all tracked repos without crashing.
* Logs clearly show progress and failed repos.
* Re-running the job is idempotent / safe.

Title: Add project view to show changes for a specific repo

Let users see “changes that affect this repo” instead of the entire global feed.

**Scope**

* UI entry point: select a tracked repo and open its project view.
* List of changes limited to registries/components used by that repo.
* Filters for severity (breaking vs all) and date range.

**Acceptance criteria**

* For a repo with usage data, project view shows a non-empty, filtered list of changes.
* Switching between different repos updates the view correctly.
* Logged-out users cannot access repo-specific views.

Title: Product risks & guardrails for shadcn/rss
Description:
## Why this issue exists

A short list of key product risks for shadcn/rss and concrete product decisions that should mitigate them.

This is not a roadmap and not full documentation — it’s “guardrails” to keep in mind when designing new features and UI.

When working on a new feature or changing an existing one — quickly skim this list.

---

## 1\. Breaking detection (errors in identifying breaking changes)

**Risk:**

We may mark a change as breaking when it isn’t, or miss a real breaking change.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] Introduce three levels instead of a boolean: `Breaking`, `Minor`, `Unknown` (with badges/colors in the UI).
- [ ] By default, show/send **only Breaking** in the feed and notifications.
- [ ] Add filters by change type (checkboxes: Breaking / Minor / Unknown).
- [ ] For each change, add a “Why” popover explaining which rules triggered the classification.
- [ ] Add a simple feedback loop on each change: buttons like `Not breaking` / `Actually breaking` (even if at first we just log it).

---

## 2\. Registry parsing/structure breaks

**Risk:**

Authors change registry structure, JSON formats, move repos — our adapters break.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] Each registry has a status: `OK` / `Degraded` / `Unknown` (visible in the UI).
- [ ] If an adapter fails, don’t break the whole system — mark the registry as `Degraded` and temporarily stop updating it.
- [ ] On the registry detail page, show a clear message, e.g.:

  ```
  “Data for this registry may be incomplete — we’re working on the adapter.”
  ```
- [ ] Have an internal (admin) view listing all registries with `Degraded/Unknown` status so they can be fixed quickly.

---

## 3\. Notification noise & fatigue (email, webhooks, GitHub issues)

**Risk:**

Too many emails, events, and issues → users will disable everything.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] Strong defaults:
  - Email: weekly digest, Breaking only.
  - Webhooks: disabled by default.
  - GitHub issues: disabled by default.
- [ ] A global “noise mode” in settings:
  - Quiet (recommended) / Normal.
- [ ] For GitHub issues: create issues for **Breaking** changes only.
- [ ] Each channel should have a clear, easy “off switch”:
  - “Pause all notifications” / “Turn off emails” / “Stop creating issues for this repo”.
- [ ] Add a simple rate limit per user/repo (e.g. max N issues per day).

---

## 4\. GitHub trust (permissions, security)

**Risk:**

People are afraid to grant GitHub access / don’t understand what we can do.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] When connecting GitHub, show a short, human-readable list: “What we can do / what we can’t do”.
- [ ] Auto-issues are enabled **only via an explicit toggle on a specific repo**.
- [ ] For each connected repo, provide a clear `Stop creating issues` button.
- [ ] Prefer a GitHub App with minimal permissions (read + create issues) over raw tokens.
- [ ] Support modes for reading code:
  * default: `Config only` (use only a config file),
  * `Light scan` — optional, not mandatory.

---

## 5\. Niche focus (only shadcn registries)

**Risk:**

Audience is too narrow → low user count.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] Make support for **trusted shadcn registries** very strong (no obvious holes or hacks).
- [ ] Design models with abstractions (DesignSystem / Registry) so we can later add other UI kits without rewriting everything.
- [ ] On pages and onboarding, gently highlight:

  ```
  “Want the same kind of tracking for other UI kits?” → link/form to collect interest.
  ```

---

## 6\. Reliability for teams (is this safe for production workflows?)

**Risk:**

Teams don’t see shadcn/rss as stable enough to build workflows on top of it.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] Version the API from day one: `/api/v1/...`.
- [ ] Label features as `Stable` / `Beta`:
  * core (feed, watchlist, notifications) → no `Beta` badge,
  * repo-aware, MCP, author dashboards → explicitly marked as `Beta` in the UI.
- [ ] Add a short “How often data updates / what we guarantee” section:
  * approximate job frequency,
  * basic expectations around stability.

---

## 7\. UX complexity (users don’t understand what’s going on)

**Risk:**

Too many concepts: registry, feed, watchlist, webhooks, repo-aware, MCP, authors.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] On the first screen, focus on **one primary scenario**:
  - “Connect GitHub → get issues when registries break.”
- [ ] Add quick presets:
  - “Follow all trusted registries”
  - “Follow registries from my project” (after GitHub is connected).
- [ ] Provide good empty states:
  - If the feed is empty → explain why and show a button “Open registry list”.
- [ ] Hide advanced features (repo-aware, MCP, author dashboards) behind separate tabs / “Advanced” sections; don’t show everything at once.

---

## 8\. Security & privacy

**Risk:**

Fear that the service reads private code, stores too much, or does something unclear.

**Product guardrails:**

- [ ] Add a simple “What data we store” section:
  * explicitly list what we store / what we definitely do not store.
- [ ] When choosing repos, enable “Show public repos only” by default.
- [ ] Provide a `Delete my account & data` button in settings (even if behind the scenes it’s an async process).
- [ ] When scanning repos, clearly show the mode:
  * “We only analyze config / only specific paths, we do not read the entire codebase.”

Title: boilerplate продаваемый
Description:
Возьми стек который ты спроектировал для wardrobe app — Next.js + Bun/Hono + Drizzle + Better Auth + Cloudflare R2 + Vercel AI SDK — и продай

его как boilerplate.

Почему именно это:

* Это современный стек который ещё плохо покрыт (create-t3-app = tRPC/Prisma/NextAuth, это старый стандарт)
* Твоя аудитория — разработчики которые именно сейчас выбирают стек для нового проекта
* Zero cold start: продукт готов сразу, не нужно набирать пользователей
* Время сборки: 3-5 недель вместо 6-12 месяцев на wardrobe app
* Wardrobe app становится demo application внутри boilerplate
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: shadcn v4 new ideas
Description:
1. ~~Preset Marketplace (Preset System)~~

Shadcn/create позволяет создавать presets с живым превью и делиться ими через коды. Идея: платформа, где дизайнеры продают presets (полные design systems) как

NFT-like коды.

Проблема с идеей: shadcn уже строит это сами через shadcn/create. Ты будешь конкурировать с самим инструментом.

Реальная ниша: вертикальные presets — shadcn-saas-preset, shadcn-dashboard-preset, shadcn-docs-preset — пакеты под конкретные типы приложений. Монетизация

через Gumroad/Lemon Squeezy.

---

2. CI/CD: Component Drift Monitor

\--diff показывает расхождение между registry и локальной версией. Идея: GitHub Action, которая на каждый PR:

* Запускает shadcn diff по всем установленным компонентам
* Показывает в PR комментарии: "Button отстал на 2 версии, вот diff"
* Блокирует merge, если critical components разошлись больше порога

Критика: Кто реально страдает от drift? Команды 5+ разработчиков с кастомизированными компонентами. Это узкий рынок, но боль острая — ты кастомизировал Button,

upstream поправил a11y баг, ты его пропустил. Проблема реальная, решение простое — можно сделать за выходные. Open source, монетизация через hosted dashboard

с историей drift.

---

3. ~~**Font Pairing Registry (done)**~~

registry:font — шрифты как first-class элемент. Идея: кураторский реестр не отдельных шрифтов, а пар и троек (heading + body + mono), уже сконфигурированных с

CSS-переменными, fallback стеками, и size/weight рекомендациями.

shadcn add font-pairing/editorial → получаешь Playfair Display + Source Serif + JetBrains Mono с настроенными scale ratios.

Критика: Google Fonts есть. [Fontpair.co](<http://Fontpair.co>) есть. Но ни один не даёт установку в один клик с правильными CSS-переменными. Это маленький, но приятный open source

проект. Монетизация сомнительна — это скорее community-building инструмент.

---

4. Monorepo Component Audit Tool (--monorepo + shadcn info)

В монорепо разные packages могут использовать разные версии компонентов. Инструмент: сканирует все packages в монорепо через shadcn info, строит матрицу

совместимости, выявляет дублирование и конфликты версий.

Реальная боль в enterprise, где есть 10+ packages в одном monorepo.

---

5. shadcn info → Project Health Dashboard

shadcn info возвращает framework, компоненты, доки. Идея: локальный веб-дашборд (think npx shadcn-health), который визуализирует:

* Какие компоненты установлены, какие доступны
* Drift от upstream (через --diff)
* Покрытие компонентами (сколько из доступных ты используешь)
* Зависимости между компонентами (граф)

---

6. ~~**MCP-сервер поверх shadcn/skills + shadcn docs (done и ждет shadcn)**~~

shadcn/skills даёт контекст агентам, shadcn docs возвращает документацию из CLI. Идея: MCP-сервер, который:

* Проксирует shadcn info → агент знает, какие компоненты уже в проекте
* Проксирует shadcn docs <component> → агент читает API-референс
* Использует shadcn/skills → агент знает паттерны и best practices
* Может выполнять shadcn add с --dry-run → агент предлагает установку, показывает diff

Результат: любой AI-инструмент (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline) работает с shadcn как native.

Критика: shadcn/skills уже интегрирован в Claude и v0. Но MCP — это стандарт, который работает везде. Сейчас каждый AI-инструмент пишет свою интеграцию.

MCP-сервер решает это раз и навсегда. Это наиболее реалистичная и полезная идея.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Premium ideas
Description:
# Монетизация fonttrio — Все варианты

## Продуктовые

### White-label Typography Registry

Приватный fonttrio-реестр под брендом компании. Разработчик делает `shadcn add https://fonts.company.com/r/brand-docs.json` и получает шрифты + type scale + CSS variables, совпадающие с Figma tokens. Dashboard для управления пэйрингами, API ключи для CI/CD, Figma sync, audit log. Тарифы: $49-299/мес per org.

### fonttrio Pro CLI

Расширение для shadcn CLI. Сканирует проект, определяет текущие шрифты, предлагает улучшения. `npx fonttrio scan` → "Your Inter + system mono pair scores 6/10. Try 'modern-clean' for better hierarchy." Бесплатный скан, платный auto-fix.

### Typography Audit SaaS

Вставляешь URL → получаешь отчёт по типографике (контраст, размеры, line-height, consistency) + рекомендацию пэйринга из fonttrio. Freemium модель.

### Lighthouse-style CLI Scoring

`npx fonttrio audit https://site.com` → Typography Score 73/100. Подсвечивает: "too many font weights loaded", "line-height too tight on mobile", "no fallback font defined". Бесплатный отчёт, платный мониторинг ($9/мес — еженедельный скан + алерты).

### Font Pairing A/B Testing

Сниппет для тестирования 2 пэйрингов на реальных юзерах. Показывает метрики: readability time, scroll depth, bounce rate. SaaS для контент-сайтов и блогов. $19-49/мес.

### Typography Tokens as a Service

Полный набор: font-face + type scale + fluid typography + responsive breakpoints + dark mode adjustments. npm-пакет `@fonttrio/tokens-editorial`. $5-15 per token set, one-time.

### Font Analytics Dashboard

"Google Analytics для типографики". Какие шрифты популярны в каких нишах, тренды, что растёт. Бесплатный публичный отчёт привлекает трафик, детальный API для агентств — платный.

---

## Плагины и интеграции

### Figma плагин

Дизайнеры выбирают пэйринг визуально в Figma → экспорт в shadcn-совместимый формат. Мост дизайн→код для типографики. \~$5-9 разово или $3/мес.

### VS Code Extension

Autocomplete для CSS typography. Пишешь `font-` → предлагает пэйринги из fonttrio с превью прямо в редакторе. Бесплатный с 6 пэйрингами, Pro ($9 one-time) с полной библиотекой.

### Storybook Addon

Переключалка пэйрингов внутри Storybook. Дизайнер ревьюит компоненты с разными пэйрингами. Бесплатный аддон → лиды на white-label.

### Webflow / Framer / WordPress плагины

No-code аудитория огромная и платит за плагины. Fonttrio picker встроенный в UI платформы. Маркетплейсы берут 30%, но дают дистрибуцию.

### Browser Extension

Показывает шрифты на любом сайте + предлагает "better pairing" из fonttrio. Freemium: бесплатный анализ, платная рекомендация + one-click apply.

### Embeddable Font Pairing Widget

`<script src="fonttrio.xyz/widget.js">` для блогов, docs-сайтов, Notion-like платформ. Бесплатный с брендингом, white-label за деньги.

---

## Партнёрские

### Vercel Integration / Marketplace

fonttrio как Vercel integration: подключил → выбрал пэйринг → автоматически настроено в проекте. Vercel платит за popular integrations.

### Интеграция в shadcn/ui

Стать "official font registry" для shadcn. Открывает путь к спонсорству от Vercel. shadcn уже имеет themes — fonts логичное расширение.

### Template Partnerships

Авторы шаблонов (Tailwind UI, shadcn themes, Envato) получают ready-made пэйринг + "install" кнопку. Revenue share: шаблон $49, fonttrio получает $5.

### Коллаборация с шрифтовыми сервисами

Fontshare, Bunny Fonts и др. хотят трафик. Платят за интеграцию своих шрифтов в fonttrio как дистрибуционный канал (CPA/CPM модель).

### Email Template Typography

Email-safe font stacks с fallbacks, протестированные в 50+ клиентах. `fonttrio add --email editorial`. Маркетинг-команды платят. $29-99 per set.

---

## Комьюнити и маркетплейс

### User-submitted Pairings Marketplace

Юзеры создают пэйринги, загружают в fonttrio. Лучшие курируются в основной реестр. Автор получает 70%, fonttrio 30%. $3-7 за пэйринг. Как Raycast extensions, но для шрифтов.

### Design Agency Directory

"Agencies that use fonttrio". Платный листинг $29-49/мес для агентств, которые хотят показать экспертизу в типографике. Трафик fonttrio → лиды для агентств.

### "Powered by fonttrio" Badge

Бесплатно с бейджом, $29 разово за удаление. Модель Crisp, Tally и подобных.

---

## Образовательные

### Интерактивный курс "Typography for Developers"

Встроен в fonttrio как `/learn`. Бесплатные базовые уроки → платный продвинутый модуль ($29-49): responsive typography, fluid scales, accessibility. Каждый урок заканчивается `shadcn add`.

### Certification Badge

"fonttrio Typography Certified". Курс + тест → бейдж для LinkedIn/GitHub. $19 за сертификацию.

### Платная рассылка "Font of the Week"

Каждую неделю разбор одного пэйринга: почему работает, где использовать, live examples. Бесплатная — 1 раз в месяц. Paid — $4/мес.

### YouTube / контент воронка

Видео "I redesigned X's typography in 5 minutes with fonttrio" → трафик → конвертация в платные продукты.

### Курс / гайд по Font Pairing

Платный PDF/видео "Font Pairing for Developers" за $19-29. fonttrio как бесплатный инструмент привлекает аудиторию, курс монетизирует экспертизу.

---

## B2B нишевые

### Accessibility Compliance Reports

Автоматическая проверка типографики на WCAG: контраст, минимальные размеры, spacing. Нужен юридическим и гос-сайтам. $49-99 per audit.

### Лицензирование датасета

Курированные пэйринги с mood/tags — structured data. AI-компании и дизайн-тулы (Canva, Figma, Framer) могут лицензировать для font recommendation engines.

### Custom Pairing API (AI)

AI-генерация пэйрингов под конкретный бренд/mood. Freemium: 3 бесплатных генерации, дальше платно.

---

## Мерч и физические продукты

### Font Pairing Poster Prints

Красиво свёрстанные постеры с пэйрингами. Print-on-demand через Printful. $19-29 за постер. Ноль затрат на инвентарь.

### Cheat Sheet Cards

Физические или PDF карточки: на одной стороне пэйринг + mood, на другой — `shadcn add` команда + use cases. Набор из 20 карточек $12.

---

## Consulting и лиды

### Consulting Leads

fonttrio как портфолио → заказы на дизайн-системы и типографику для компаний.

### API для SaaS-билдеров

Website builders, CMS, Framer-like тулы встраивают fonttrio font picker в свой UI. Платный API по запросам. Модель Unsplash API.

Title: Primitives. Text Animations
Description:
'- [ ] FadeText.Компонент появления текста с fade. Props: text, duration, delay. Реализация через useCurrentFrame() + interpolate() opacity
- [ ] BlurText.Появление текста из размытия. Props: text, duration, delay, blurAmount. Анимация filter: blur() через interpolate.
- [ ] SplitText. Побуквенная/пословная анимация появления. Props: text, splitBy ("char" | "word"), duration, stagger.
- [ ] TypeWriter.Эффект печатающегося текста. Props: text, speed, cursor.
- [ ] CountUp. Анимация счётчика чисел. Props: from, to, duration, format.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Primitives - Element Animations
Description:
'- [ ] FadeIn. Универсальный fade вход для children. Props: duration, delay, direction (опционально — fade + slide)
- [ ] SlideIn.Вход со стороны. Props: direction ("left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom"), duration, delay, distance.
- [ ] ScaleIn. Вход с масштабированием. Props: initialScale, duration, delay.
- [ ] RotateIn. Вход с вращением. Props: degrees, duration, delay.
Creator: kapishdima@gmail.com

Title: Primitives - Transitions

- [ ] Wipe. Wipe transition. Props: direction ("left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom"), durationInFrames.
- [ ] SlideTransition. Slide между сценами. Props: direction, durationInFrames.
- [ ]

Title: Compositions
Description:
'- [ ] IntroScene. Сцена-интро: лого + заголовок + background. Использует FadeIn, FadeText, GradientBackground. Props: logo, title, subtitle,background.
- [ ] FeatureScene. Сцена демонстрации фичи: скриншот + текст. Использует SlideIn, FadeText. Props: image, title, description, layout ("left" |   "right").
- [ ] OutroScene. Финальная сцена: CTA + ссылка + лого. Использует FadeIn, FadeText. Props: logo, cta, url.

Title: shadcn cli remove command
Description:
* New `shadcn remove [components...]` command - the inverse of `add`
* Deletes a component's own files only. Does not touch `package.json`, `globals.css`, `tailwind.config.*`, or transitive `registryDependencies`
* Prints follow-up notices listing which registry deps and npm packages might now be orphaned, with a ready-to-copy follow-up command

## Motivation

`shadcn add` installs components into a user's project, but there is currently no inverse operation. To remove a component, users have to manually delete files and remember which paths each component touched (UI files, hooks, blocks, registry pages). This command closes that loop. 
The implementation is intentionally minimal: it reuses the same registry resolver and file-path planner that `add` uses, so it inherits support for all component types (`registry:ui`, `registry:hook`, `registry:component`, `registry:block`, `registry:page`, `registry:lib`, `registry:file`) without type-specific branching

## Usage

```bash
shadcn remove button
shadcn remove button card input
shadcn remove https://example.com/r/styles/new-york-v4/login-01.json
shadcn remove @ncdai/github-contributions
shadcn remove button --dry-run
shadcn remove @ncdai/github-contributions --force
```

Behavior

1. Validates arguments - empty list exits with a friendly error
2. Loads components.json (errors if missing)
3. Fetches the requested items from the registry (handles names + URLs)
4. Validates every target path through isSafeTarget the same security guard add uses against path-traversal payloads
5. Plans the absolute paths via resolveFilePath + findCommonRoot (same utilities add uses)
6. Critical-files guard: blocks deletion of `tailwind.config.*, globals.css, app/globals.css, lib/utils.ts` unless `--force` is set.
7. Deletes each existing planned path (fs.unlink wrapped in try/catch)
8. Prints a deletion summary, a warning per component whose files were all absent (likely installed with a custom --path), and info notices listing transitive `registryDependencies` and merged npm dependencies

The info notice for registry deps now includes a ready-to-copy follow-up:

```
The following registry components were installed as dependencies they may still be used elsewhere in your project. Check before removing them: button, primitive-dialog

To remove them too, run:
shadcn remove button primitive-dialog
```

What it does NOT do:

* No npm dependency removal. Components share npm packages (e.g. lucide-react); a reverse-use analysis is needed to know whether removal is safe. Surfaced as an info notice instead
* No CSS variable / Tailwind config cleanup. Same reason - shared state.
* No transitive registryDependencies cascade. A dialog removal doesn't auto-remove button, because button is often used by other installed components. The info notice gives the user a copy-paste command if they want to remove them
* No import scanning in user code. Deleting a component may break references elsewhere. This is left to the user / typecheck / linter to surface

Known limitations

* Components installed with a custom --path are detected as "no files found" and skipped with a warning  the files at the custom path are not deleted
* Empty directories left behind are not cleaned up

Title: v3
Description:
Изменяем основной акцент на шрифт, не шрифтовые пары, а одиночные шрифты. Для этого делаем так: 

1. Убираем landing page
2. Делаем чтобы страница шрифтов была root
3. Переделываем ui страницы шрифтов
4. Страницу шрифтовых пар оставляем, но так же переделываем

Нужно сделать страницу шрифтов такой же как в fonts.google.com, а именно сами карточки шрифтов - в ряд, не сеткой. При нажатии открываем страницу, а не модалку. Слева должны быть фильтры, которые по-дефолту закрыты, сверху должен быть поиск
Контейнер должен быть таким, чтобы сбоку всегда было место для спонсоров на любой страница

В фильтрах добавляем возможность ввести текст который примениться ко всем шрифтам

На странице одного шрифта показываем все как в specimen, только без большого редактора
Нужна command block installation для того чтобы можно было скопировать шрифт, также добавить в "корзину" можно выбрать несколько шрифтов и добавить их
